sections.literary-appearances: Romance of the Three Kingdoms: 114, 119–120Jin Shu: 3 [b]
sections.biography-source-undefined:
Sima Yan was styled Anshi. He was the eldest son of Sima Zhao by Wang Yuanji.

During the Jiaping era, he was enfeoffed as District Marquis of Beiping.

Some time after 249, he was made General of the Interior Camp.

Following the death of Emperor Cao Mao in 260, Sima Yan personally received Cao Huan at Dongwuyang, who would later be made the new emperor. It appears that this earned him a promotion to Township Marquis of Xinchang.

He was known to be generous and benevolent, but also too tolerating.

full_text:
Sima Yan (Anshi) 司馬炎 (安世)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Sima Yan (司馬炎), styledAnshi (安世). Overthrew Wei and founded Jin. He oversaw the conquest of Wu. Sima Yan was affiliated with the Wei Kingdom and the Jin Dynasty. Sima Yan lived during the Three Kingdoms and Jin Dynasty periods of Chinese history.

Sima Yan bio
BiographySource Undefined
Sima Yan was styled Anshi. He was the eldest son of Sima Zhao by Wang Yuanji.
During the Jiaping era, he was enfeoffed as District Marquis of Beiping.
Some time after 249, he was made General of the Interior Camp.
Following the death of Emperor Cao Mao in 260, Sima Yan personally received Cao Huan at Dongwuyang, who would later be made the new emperor. It appears that this earned him a promotion to Township Marquis of Xinchang.
He was known to be generous and benevolent, but also too tolerating.
